name: Transport details to BCP (port of entry + arrival leg)
pageTitle: Transport to the port of entry - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
caption: Transport
heading: Transport to the port of entry
layout: govuk-grid-row > govuk-grid-column-two-thirds (with a nested govuk-grid-column-full used for the container/trailer grid)

structure.text: Transport to the port of entry
Means of transport to port of entry
Select means of transport to port of entry / Airplane / Railway / Road vehicle / Vessel

To identify the means of transport, enter (one of the following): flight number / train number / road vehicle registration number / vessel name (for ferries, also the road vehicle registration number)
Are any road trailers or shipping containers being used to transport the consignment?
Container or trailer number / Seal number / Official seal / Add another container or trailer
structure.govukComponent: Conditionally revealed text inputs + checkbox + link-button (revealed on Yes)

structure.text: Enter the reference number on the air waybill, bill of lading, sea waybill, road consignment note (CMR) or other transport document.
Estimated arrival at port of entry

structure.govukComponent: Date input (govuk-date-input) enhanced with a custom defra-datepicker

structure.text: Hour / Minutes (Enter the time using 24 hour format, for example, 14 50.)
structure.govukComponent: Date-input-style two-field time entry (govuk-date-input reused for time)

govukComponents.usage: Page H1 'Transport to the port of entry'
'Means of transport to port of entry' and 'Estimated arrival at port of entry' section headings
Port of entry (BCP, autocomplete-enhanced) and Means of transport dropdown
Transport identification, container number, seal number, official seal, transport document reference, date/time hints
Transport identification, container/trailer number, seal number, transport document reference, and date/time part inputs
'Are any road trailers or shipping containers being used...' Yes/No with conditional reveal
'Official seal' checkbox inside the revealed container/trailer block
Groups the road-trailers radios, the date entry and the time entry
Estimated arrival date (Day/Month/Year) and estimated arrival time (Hour/Minutes) both built on the date-input component
'Save and return to hub' and 'Save and continue'
'Cancel and return to hub' link to notification overview

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: autocomplete__wrapper, autocomplete__input, autocomplete__menu, autocomplete__option, autocomplete__status (with visible input#bcp layered over hidden select#bcp-select)
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: accessible-autocomplete (alphagov) enhances the Port of entry <select> into a type-ahead combobox — the trace shows the user typing 'TILBURY (GBTIL)' and picking an option.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Third-party JS widget outside the core govuk-frontend component set; degrades to a plain select without JS. Adds ARIA live-region status nodes (aria-live-message).
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-frontend has no autocomplete, but accessible-autocomplete is the DfE/GDS-endorsed companion. The new app can keep it for the BCP list (reference data), or fall back to a plain Select for a small list.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: defra-datepicker, date-picker__container, date-picker__dialog, date-picker__reveal__icon, date-picker__button__previous-month/next-month/close, date-picker__date-table, date-picker__heading
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A bespoke DEFRA calendar date-picker overlaying the govuk-date-input, revealed by a 'Choose date' icon button; shows a month grid ('July 2026') with previous/next-month and cancel controls.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Fully custom widget — not part of govuk-frontend. Significant bespoke JS/CSS and its own accessibility surface. This is the classic IPAFFS date-picker overlay.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: GOV.UK Design System recommends a plain govuk-date-input (three text fields, no calendar) for known dates. The new app should default to the bare date-input and drop the calendar overlay unless there is a proven user need.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 119 (class inventory + structure outline: 'Choose date', 'Previous month', 'July 2026', 'Next month', 'Cancel')

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Custom layout class wrapping the revealed container/trailer number + seal number + official seal fields (a repeatable row set with 'Add another container or trailer').
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Bespoke grid, not a govuk layout utility; drives the repeatable container/trailer sub-form.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Rebuild the repeatable block with govuk grid rows/columns or a govuk-summary-list 'add another' pattern; no custom grid class needed.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A <button> styled as a link — the 'Add another container or trailer' control.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Non-standard button/link hybrid; govuk-frontend has no link-button class.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Use a govuk-button--secondary or a plain govuk-link styled action for 'Add another', per the GOV.UK 'add another' pattern.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 119 (structure outline: BUTTON.link-button 'Add another container or trailer')

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Modifier on the 'Means of transport to port of entry' h2, presumably to attach inline help.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: IPAFFS-specific heading treatment, not a govuk class.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Use govuk-heading-m plus govuk-hint, or a Details component for help text.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Screen-reader-only text, an ARIA live-region message node (from the autocomplete), and a hidden-toggle utility.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Minor utility classes; sr-only duplicates govuk-visually-hidden (which is also present), a small redundancy.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Standardise on govuk-visually-hidden; live-region handling comes from the autocomplete library.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Extra per-part classes on the arrival date and time inputs, used to wire up the custom datepicker and the time sub-fields.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Custom hooks layered on the govuk-date-input; the time entry (Hour/Minutes) reuses the date-input component rather than a dedicated time component.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Plain govuk-date-input for the date; for time, two govuk-input width-2 fields in a fieldset (GOV.UK has no time component) — no custom part-classes required.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 119 (class inventory + control inventory)

fields.name: bcp (select id=bcp-select; visible autocomplete input id=bcp)
fields.label: Port of entry
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 119 (full 34-option list captured incl. leading 'Select port of entry' placeholder; re-verified by enumerating bcp-select.options). Reference data — the BCP/port list should come from a reference-data service, not be hardcoded. TILBURY (GBTIL) IS present in the captured list (option 32) and was the value typed and selected via the accessible-autocomplete at actions 115-116 (an EARLIER correction wrongly claimed TILBURY was absent / the list filtered TILBURY out — that is false; the whole 34-port list including TILBURY rendered). CORROBORATION (inferred): tests/notification/ched-p/ched-p-poe-validation.spec.ts:34-88 proves the port list is filtered PER COUNTRY OF ORIGIN, not by commodity — the UI list = all 'real' POEs plus 'dummy' POEs, with Scotland (GBSCOT) dummy port EXCLUDED for EU origins (France) but INCLUDED for Northern Ireland, and dummy ports excluded entirely for other non-EU origins (Angola). Real-vs-dummy split comes from getPortsSeparated(request,'CVEDP') via bip-workflows. The port list is enhanced by accessible-autocomplete over select[aria-label='Port of entry'] and is read in tests via getAllPortOfEntryOptions (TransportDetailsPage.ts:114-117). HOLYHEAD (GBHLY) is asserted available as a Port of entry for EU CHED-P (tests/notification/ched-p/ched-p-holyhead.spec.ts:28-39). DEFAULT_CONFIG port is TILBURY (GBTIL) (ched-p-workflows.ts:171).

fields.name: transport-means-before
fields.label: Choose from:
options: Select means of transport to port of entry
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 117 (select) + action 119 snapshot; option values plane/rail/road/ship. CORROBORATION: the four underlying option values are the transportMethod domain enum airplane='plane', railway='rail', roadVehicle='road', vessel='ship' (types/transport-method.ts:1-6). The workflow drives this via dropdownChooseFrom.selectOption(transportMethod) — getByLabel('Choose from:') (TransportDetailsPage.ts:16-18; ched-p-workflows.ts:422). DEFAULT_CONFIG transportMethod = plane (ched-p-workflows.ts:172).

fields.name: identification
fields.label: Transport identification
fields.hint: To identify the means of transport, enter (one of the following): flight number; train number; road vehicle registration number; vessel name (for ferries, also the road vehicle registration number)
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 118 (fill) + action 119 snapshot (hint list)

fields.name: consignment-in-container (radios id are-consignments-in-containers-yes / are-consignments-in-containers-no)
fields.label: Are any road trailers or shipping containers being used to transport the consignment?
fields.hint: Provide details of all road trailers and containers or your consignment may be delayed. You may need to contact your haulier for this.
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 119 snapshot (radio group, 'No' checked)

fields.name: container-number-1
fields.label: Container or trailer number
fields.hint: Enter the container identification number, or the trailer registration number or number plate.
fields.conditionalOn: 'Are any road trailers or shipping containers being used to transport the consignment?' = Yes (govuk-radios--conditional reveal). Repeatable via 'Add another container or trailer'.
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 119 (control inventory container-number-1 + hint container-number-1-hint; revealed block, not exercised in this trace as 'No' was chosen)

fields.name: seal-number-1
fields.label: Seal number
fields.hint: Enter the seal number on the official certificate or any other seal mentioned in the accompanying documents.
fields.conditionalOn: 'Are any road trailers or shipping containers being used...' = Yes
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 119 (control inventory seal-number-1 + hint seal-number-1-hint)

fields.name: official-seal-1
fields.label: Official seal
fields.hint: An official seal is affixed under the supervision of the competent authority issuing the certificate.
fields.conditionalOn: 'Are any road trailers or shipping containers being used...' = Yes
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 119 (control inventory official-seal-1 checkbox + hint official-seal-1-hint; checkbox visible label rendered as 'official-seal-1' in structure but the field legend/label is 'Official seal')

fields.name: document
fields.label: Transport document reference
fields.hint: Enter the reference number on the air waybill, bill of lading, sea waybill, road consignment note (CMR) or other transport document.
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 119 (fill 'certificate'; hint document-hint; govuk-input govuk-!-width-three-quarters)

fields.name: arrival-date-day / arrival-date-month / arrival-date-year
fields.label: Estimated arrival at port of entry
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d actions 120-122 (Day 18, Month 07, Year 2026) + action 119 snapshot (govuk-date-input Day/Month/Year, enhanced by custom defra-datepicker 'Choose date')

fields.name: arrival-time-hour / arrival-time-minutes
fields.label: Time of estimated arrival
fields.hint: Enter the time using 24 hour format, for example, 14 50. If you are unsure of the exact time: Avoid using times on the hour, for example, 00 00; For non-GVMS route, update the time when you know the time of arrival.
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d actions 123-124 (Hour 14, Minutes 00) + action 119 snapshot (Hour/Minutes two-field time entry built on govuk-date-input). Default arrivalTime = { hour: '14', minute: '00' } (ched-p-workflows.ts:177-180). Filled via fillArrivalTime -> inputArrivalTimeHour getByRole('textbox',{name:'Hour',exact:true}) + inputArrivalTimeMinutes getByRole('textbox',{name:'Minutes'}) (TransportDetailsPage.ts:55-61,92-95).

fields.name: bcp-or-port-of-entry (variant combobox, label 'BCP or Port of entry')
fields.label: BCP or Port of entry
fields.conditionalOn: Variant of the port field shown on a different transport-page branch (entry-BCP / CTC / GVMS flow) rather than the plain 'Port of entry' seen in our trace. Not exercised by any trace in our corpus.
fields.evidence: TransportDetailsPage.ts:12-14 (inputBcpOrPortOfEntry = getByRole('combobox',{name:'BCP or Port of entry'})) + :119-124 (selectBcpOrPortOfEntry typeahead). The GVMS/entry-BCP workflow drives this branch with bcpOrPortOfEntry defaulting to LONDON GATEWAY, a GVMS port (ched-p-workflows.ts:595-596,657,763). This is the SAME control as 'Port of entry' but relabelled when the journey is a Common-Transit / GVMS route — a page-variant a happy-path trace never rendered.

fields.name: entry-border-control-post (variant select, label 'Entry border control post')
fields.label: Entry border control post
fields.conditionalOn: Variant-only: appears on an entry-BCP branch of the transport page (not exercised in our corpus). selectRandomEntryBcp picks a non-empty option.
fields.evidence: TransportDetailsPage.ts:20-22 (dropdownEntryBorderControlPost = getByLabel('Entry border control post')) + :149-166 (selectRandomEntryBcp enumerates option[value] non-empty). Reference-data-driven BCP list; no trace rendered it.

fields.name: inspection-premises (variant select, label 'Inspection premises')
fields.label: Inspection premises
fields.conditionalOn: Variant-only: a further-refinement dropdown that can appear alongside the entry BCP (e.g. a specific inspection point within a port). Not exercised in our corpus.
fields.evidence: TransportDetailsPage.ts:24-26 (dropdownInspectionPremises = getByLabel('Inspection premises')). Page object exposes it but no workflow or trace fills it — required-ness unknown.

fields.name: means-of-transport-after-bcp-or-poe (variant select, label 'Means of transport after BCP or Port of entry')
fields.label: Means of transport after BCP or Port of entry
fields.conditionalOn: Variant label for the means-of-transport dropdown on the combined/entry-BCP branch. In our trace the equivalent control was labelled 'Choose from:'. Not exercised as this label in our corpus.
fields.evidence: TransportDetailsPage.ts:36-38 (dropdownMeansOfTransport = getByLabel('Means of transport after BCP or Port of entry')). A relabelled variant of the transport-means select; underlying values are the transportMethod enum (plane/rail/road/ship).

fields.name: estimated-journey-time-hours (variant text, label 'Hours')
fields.label: Estimated journey time (Hours)
fields.conditionalOn: Variant-only: an estimated-journey-time-in-hours field that appears on certain transport branches (e.g. onward/GVMS legs). Not rendered in our trace.
fields.evidence: TransportDetailsPage.ts:63-65 (inputEstimatedJourneyTimeHours = getByRole('textbox',{name:'Hours',exact:true})). Exposed by the page object but never filled by a ched-p workflow or captured in a trace — required-ness and exact label/hint unknown.

validationMessages.message: You cannot enter a date more than 30 days in the past or 180 days in the future
validationMessages.evidence: tests/notification/ched-p/ched-p-date-validations.spec.ts:9 (REJECTION_MESSAGE const) + :65 (expect linkValidationErrors toContainText). The test matrix at :11-24 pins the accepted window as -30 days..+180 days INCLUSIVE: -29/-30 accepted, -31 rejected; +179/+180 accepted, +182 rejected. Applies identically for EU (France) and non-EU (Afghanistan) countries of origin. The page-object error-summary link is matched by getByRole('link',{name:'You cannot enter a date more'}) — TransportDetailsPage.ts:78-80. No trace in our corpus rendered this message (this page's trace had zero errors), so it is a test-asserted rule, not observed reality.

validationMessages.message: (exact copy not asserted — first inline error text read from #error-description-0)
validationMessages.evidence: TransportDetailsPage.ts:82-84 exposes firstErrorMessage = locator('#error-description-0'), i.e. the page renders a GOV.UK error-summary with anchored inline messages, but no test asserts the copy for the non-date fields (port of entry, means of transport, transport identification, transport document reference, arrival time). The mandatory-field validation copy for those fields is unknown — a human question / an error-state trace is needed.

openQuestions: Required-ness for each field is inferred from the page role, not from an observed validation error — this trace had zero errors, so no error summary or inline messages were captured. An error-state trace should be mined to confirm the exact validation copy and which fields are mandatory (particularly seal number and official seal, which may be optional).

The captured BCP <select> held all 34 options INCLUDING TILBURY (GBTIL) (option 32), which the user typed and selected at actions 115-116. The port list is still a reference-data concern — the corroborating tests (see the bcp field evidence) show it is filtered per country of origin (real POEs always, dummy POEs conditionally). The new app should source the port/BCP list from a reference-data service parameterised by CHED type + origin.

The 'Are any road trailers or shipping containers being used...' Yes branch reveals a repeatable container/trailer sub-form (Container or trailer number, Seal number, Official seal, 'Add another container or trailer'). This trace chose 'No', so the revealed fields were captured from the DOM but never filled — a Yes-path trace should be mined to confirm repeat behaviour and per-row field names beyond -1.

The custom defra-datepicker calendar overlay and the accessible-autocomplete on Port of entry are the two significant non-govuk widgets; both are IPAFFS-specific enhancements the rebuild should reconsider (plain govuk-date-input; keep autocomplete only for the long BCP list).

This page is IPAFFS's CHED-P (fish) transport page reached at .../transport/before-bip — it is the 'before BCP' leg. Actions 126-134 in the same trace show a SECOND means-of-transport leg (another Choose from / Transport identification / document / date / time) plus a follow-on page with 'Are you using the Common...' and 'Will the transport use the...' radios (actions 135-136) — i.e. onward-transport / GVMS questions that likely belong to adjacent pages, not this one.

CORROBORATED (from tests): the arrival-date validation window is -30 days..+180 days inclusive, same for EU and non-EU origins, message 'You cannot enter a date more than 30 days in the past or 180 days in the future' (ched-p-date-validations.spec.ts). The IUU rebuild should carry this rule but the exact boundary semantics (is it calendar days or 24h windows? relative to submission or to 'now'?) are not pinned by the tests — a human should confirm for the new service.

CORROBORATED (from tests): the Port of entry list is filtered by COUNTRY OF ORIGIN (real POEs always; dummy POEs conditionally; Scotland GBSCOT dummy excluded for EU except Northern Ireland) — not by commodity as originally guessed. This confirms the list MUST come from a reference-data service parameterised by CHED type + origin, and that 'dummy' test ports exist in the data. The IUU service needs the equivalent real/dummy + origin filtering rules defined.

The page object exposes several controls NO trace in our corpus rendered, added here as inferred variant-only fields: 'BCP or Port of entry' (relabelled port field on the CTC/GVMS entry-BCP branch), 'Entry border control post' select, 'Inspection premises' select, 'Means of transport after BCP or Port of entry' (relabelled means select), and an 'Estimated journey time (Hours)' text field. A human should confirm which of these belong on the IUU transport page vs adjacent pages, and their required-ness and copy — an entry-BCP-branch trace would settle it.

The page object also exposes btnSaveAndReview ('Save and review') alongside 'Save and continue' / 'Save and return to hub' (TransportDetailsPage.ts:74-76). This appears on the amend/review path (used by ched-p-workflows.ts:1328). Confirm whether the IUU page needs a distinct 'Save and review' action for amend journeys.
